text.text:
Dining at Centurion New York was nothing short of a dream. The moment you step inside, it feels as if you’re tasting—just for an instant—the exhilarating, almost surreal dimension of New York itself. The atmosphere, the design, the energy… everything comes together to create an unforgettable experience.

A very special recognition goes to Chef Rodrigo, whose culinary mastery elevated the evening to another level. Each dish was better than the one before it, crafted with precision, creativity, and a deep understanding of flavor. Truly remarkable.

We also want to highlight Gabriel, our server, who brought a warm, familiar welcome to the table. His elegance, professionalism, and genuine attentiveness reflected the high standards of a place like this. The entire staff operated with the same level of excellence—gracious, polished, and perfectly aligned with the spirit of Centurion.

This was more than a dinner; it was an experience. One that captures the magic of New York in every detail. We left deeply impressed and grateful.

text.text:
I can for sure tell that this was one of the best dining experiences in New York. I had been looking for so long for a special place to celebrate my friend's belated birthday, and I'm so happy that we booked here.

Amazing food, fresh and tasty, so nicely presented; all details were impeccable. To start, we ordered tuna tartare, carrot arancini, stracciatella and fig salad, a selection of cheese, and kale salad, and everything was delightful. Even though we were already full, we ordered mushroom pasta with cognac puree with a side of delicata squash and, of course, French fries—also super tasty. We finished our experience with a very refreshing dessert, Chestnut Mont Blanc, and if you are a huge fan of extremely sweet desserts, the chocolate eclair would be a great choice.

Amazing ambiance, outstanding view, and calm music made our dining experience great. We are definitely looking forward to our next visit, probably to try their pre-fixe menu experience.

Regular NY prices, 2 of us spent $500 together with tip.

(They are not refreshing the table between meals, meaning not crumbing, and that is the only thing that bothers me a bit.)

candidates.formattedAddress: 112 W 25th St Floor 38 & 39, Manhattan, NY 10001, USA

text.text:
Amazing Night at Somewhere Nowhere!

I had such a great time at the Somewhere Nowhere comedy show — the comedians were genuinely funny, the energy was high, and the whole atmosphere felt intimate and effortless.

I also have to mention the matcha chocolate mousse and the churros with the chocolate on the side — absolutely delicious. The perfect mix of light, sweet, and comforting.

After the show I went up to the rooftop and the view took the night to another level. Beautiful skyline, great music, and such a good vibe overall.

A perfect experience from start to finish. Highly recommend!

Magical rooftop!
This rooftop is an absolute gem! The service was warm and attentive from start to finish, the food was outstanding with every dish full of flavor, and the atmosphere was simply perfect—relaxed yet vibrant. Cocktails are great, yummy cosmopolitan 🍸 What truly sets it apart is the view: the Empire State Building on one side, the Hudson River sunset on the other. It’s hands down one of the best spots in the city to enjoy an unforgettable evening. Can’t wait to come back!

text.text: The management at this establishment is truly incompetent. At first, they wouldn't let me in because I had a bag with me. I told them I could pay a fee and leave my bag at coat check, but they told me they didn't allow it. Then I left my bag at work and came back. When I went up to the rooftop and used the small camera I had to record the sunset, the security guard came and told me that shooting with cameras other than cell phones was prohibited. The biggest plus of this place is the view. If someone doesn't take beautiful photos of the view and share it, how will people find out and come here? The business rules are ridiculous and unreasonable. When you sit inside, you can't see clearly through the plastic mat. When you sit outside, it's difficult to see the full view from the seats and trees. Also, their food isn't very good.  I ordered the CHIMICHURRI CROQUETTES and they tasted pretty bad. The WAGYU BEEF SLIDERS were also pretty bland.

Our expectation was less looking at the other reviews but to our surprise this spot was cool and had the vibe. Brunch is kids friendly which was great. Hence to skip the driving booked a room there for 2 days but didn’t realise there were different Moxy hotels in NYC. Had to drive and pay for parking separately.

Wasn’t over crowded since it was afternoon hour. My kid enjoyed & so did we!

The pina colada drink was good but tasted more on the lemon side. Did sit on a high seat which was kinda uncomfortable. If I had a normal sitting table maybe my experience would be much better. Made a reservation on open table. Cool open rooftop area. Overall was a good brunch day for pictures. This is somewhere I came once and I’m good so won’t be coming back. Go for the vibes and the atmosphere. One and done experience.

text.text:
Magic Hour Rooftop is truly one of a kind! Such a beautiful spot with a stunning carousel decorated with flowers – it feels absolutely magical. The drinks are just wow, and I definitely recommend trying the cocktail bouquet, especially the biggest one – it’s an experience in itself.

The food is very good, though the portions are on the smaller side, which is fine for this type of place. What I really love is how the vibe changes depending on where you sit: on one side it feels more like a lively lunch party with a DJ, and on the other side it’s romantic with a more intimate atmosphere. The DJ playing during my last visit was fantastic and really added to the night (@djkizzy)

I’ve been here several times already, and everyone I’ve ever brought absolutely loved it. I’ll definitely keep coming back – highly recommend!

text.text:
Hard PASS. After a long waited 3 ish years of waiting to visit Magic Hour, I was finally able to make my trip align with visiting Magic Hour during their themed seasons!

You all posted on social media stating the new winter theme would be up by Nov 26 and to click the link in bio to make a reservation to come see the new set up. I clicked. I reserved. I came & saw nothing.

Of course once my party gets there for our reservation, we get told “it’s getting set up”…that’s not what it says online but fine. We sit and wait, probably close to 10 mins before our waitress approaches us….. but no problem they could be busy with the 3 small groups of people already seated… Let’s enjoy happy hour and move on. Happy hour is 3 drinks and 3 drinks only…. Okay sure, let’s get the holiday drink featured on social media that also appears on the happy hour menu and the actual menu as the same name. I look at the drink I order and it looks completely different. Confused I asked about the cranberry & rosemary, she said “sure” as if to grab these things for me to make it myself. She comes back with cranberries in a cup and pours them into my drink……….um? I asked again, “No it’s different” is what the waitress tells me. If the holiday drink has the same name on happy hour (Winter White Spritz) why am I getting a completely different drink? The waitress was super nice the entire experience but I think the language barrier really played a part in not understanding my frustration with the entire situation.

I asked the waitress if there was anything else to the bar because the social media page makes it seem like there are many different areas and get told no. Come to find out there are three different bars for Magic Hour and though we asked several times about the winter theme for today, no one seems concerned or cared for the fact that this is the only reason I made this reservation as they POSTED online about it being launched on the 26th…the day they stated to click the link to make a reservation for to view the new theme.

The false advertisement on both ends for the drinks and the winter set up completion was a complete disappointment.

The only great thing we did get to “experience” were the sliders. The chicken & ribeye sliders were delicious, but not worth the trouble we went through while being there.

Disappointed to say the least. On top of watching other parties that came after us get serviced quickly and with excitement, it was such a drag to be there as much as we tried to enjoy the moment. The time it took to be serviced versus those after us. The same drink but not the same drink between the happy hour menu and regular menu. They forgot one of the food orders we placed and was confused on whether we even ordered it…we did. & the lack of understanding the frustration when being asked about the area, the space and the theme we only showed up for. It’s a great concept, but it seemed like you needed to already know someone that worked there to truly get a great experience.

text.text:
The place is stunning — the food and atmosphere are truly five-star quality, and it’s clear that most of the staff work hard to make guests feel welcome.
However, our experience at the bar didn’t match the same level of care. The bartender seemed distracted and lacked the empathy and attention one expects in such a highly rated venue.
I came here with my wife to enjoy what’s known as one of the best rooftop spots in the city, and it’s a shame that one person’s attitude can affect an otherwise wonderful experience.
This place has everything to be perfect if all staff shared the same level of professionalism as the rest of the team.

text.text:
To be honest, this place is really good. I read some of the bad reviews, and it seems like people are expecting VIP service or something high-end. But for what you’re paying, those expectations are a bit much.

What I really liked about this place, especially compared to so many other spots in NYC, are the prices. In this area, the average price for a cocktail is around $20, but here, they keep it below that—even for a speakeasy. And the cocktails are genuinely tasty. I tried the sliders and croquettes, and both were delicious. The space itself isn’t huge, which gives it a cozy, warm vibe.

As for the entertainment, there’s someone singing classic jazz songs. It’s a nice touch—not a huge show, but they don’t charge extra for it, unlike other places.

Overall, it’s a great spot. My friend and I had a really good time, and I even came back for a date. It’s not a party scene, but it’s perfect for a date night or a relaxed catch-up with friends.

text.text:
We came here with a group of 9 on a Saturday night with a reservation. The waitstaff/busboys were great, no complaints about them. The karaoke system was fine too. HOWEVER there were a couple of major issues:

1. Randos being allowed into your room from the club level (downstairs) - there was no one preventing people from the club section downstairs from coming upstairs to the karaoke section. This led to our room being interrupted by random strangers TWO separate times. The second time, a drunk guy came in, grabbed a mic, and spilled an entire pitcher of juice all over me - and then he wouldn’t leave until the guys in our group literally wrestled him out of the room. They came from the club floor. It’s ridiculous that they even had unchecked access to our room (mind you we spent well over 1k for 3 hrs here).
2. Long ass bathroom lines (as a result of point #1) - there were two single-stall bathroom lines for the karaoke floor. Every time someone from our group went to the bathroom, there was already a line of 5+ drunk girls/guys, making every bathroom break take at least 10 minutes. The cherry on top is that one of the bathrooms didn’t even flush 🤢

There are so many other options in Ktown, would NOT recommend this place.

text.text: I came in on my birthday yesterday to celebrate. I always show people warmth and respect but I feel I did not receive the same when I came in. I was the first to arrive in my group of 5 I booked the room for us. When I was checking in the host showed me to my room without telling me how the room and rates are. It wasn’t till I called him back to the room to ask how I was being charged. $45 for the room fee and $10 drink min pp per hour. I arrived in my room at 9:33pm ordered my shots a sprite and began enjoying my karaoke session. The host came knocking at 10:15 saying that the song I was singing would be my last song. I was confused because I thought I was paying per hour. When I asked why because I understood Im paying for the room and my friends haven’t showed yet. He explained it’s because there is another reservation and also said I have the room for 40 minutes not an hour (not clear) and I didn’t meet my drink minimum. I then expressed that I did not feel like things were explained clearly to me in which he replied “ I explained everything perfectly clear”. I disagree. I then asked him to charge me shots for the drink minimum so I can enjoy more time. I then asked if I could stay more time now that requirements have been met or do I still have to leave for your next reservation, he said I could stay no problem I can stay. So I’m assuming the “reservation “ was a way to push me out? I continued to karaoke and enjoy my birthday. My friends arrived at 11:15. We ordered more shots and drinks to hit our minimum. The host came back to ask if we wanted more time at 11:30 and his whole attitude had changed. Way nicer and welcoming when my friends arrived. I said NO no more time needed. Even though I wanted to stay more the way I was treated was not comfortable for me. We paid tipped and I did say to the host I will not be back. This was my first time at this karaoke spot and I’m so disheartened and disappointed by the poor hospitality. There was one nice bartender who brought us our last round of drinks I did not get his name but he was the sweetest and I’m so grateful for his great service! Other than that never coming back.*ps if you’re going to talk trash about your customers don’t do so close to the rooms that they’re in…we can hear you*

text.text:
Visited Karaoke K last night with a party of three. The private karaoke room was priced at $40 per hour, with a minimum drink requirement of $30 per person (excluding food and snacks). Since one member of our group doesn’t drink, the remaining two of us had to order approximately six to seven drinks to meet the $90 total minimum. Unfortunately, one of the drinks never arrived, and the rest were served during the last 15 minutes of our session.

Our reservation was scheduled for 10:30 p.m., but upon arrival, we were informed that it was listed as 11:30 p.m., which did not match the confirmation email we received. However, the staff accommodated us by providing the next available room.

Our total came to approximately $164. While the rooms were really nice and the sound system was excellent, the overall experience was disappointing due to the strict drink requirement and the slow service. Regrettably, we don’t plan to return.

text.text:
Circo is a cool venue, clean and stylish, but watch out for the Preheater Bar downstairs—it’s a total rip-off! I was charged $39 for a single glass of Macallan 12, which felt outrageous. To make it worse, the receipt sneakily listed the drink as a “double,” even though we only ordered singles. Very shady.

We attended the Halloween burlesque variety show, which was actually quite entertaining! One of the dancers had a pretty wild wardrobe malfunction, leaving her completely exposed—definitely memorable. The host stole the show, giving off serious Chris Tucker-in-The Fifth Element vibes and keeping the crowd laughing throughout the night.

While the vibe at Circo is generally great, you need to be super cautious about their sketchy tab practices. They automatically add a 20% tip to your bill, which is fine—until you go to scan the QR code and check out, where they sneak in an extra 20-30% tip on top! If you’re not careful, you’ll end up doubling your tip. Make sure to adjust the tip to 0% at checkout to avoid being scammed. Super scummy dishonest and shady—Circo, you can do better!

text.text:
Sandbar Rooftop in Chelsea is one of the best rooftop bars in NYC if you’re looking for incredible skyline views. You can see the Empire State Building and all the surrounding Manhattan skyscrapers — it’s honestly one of the most photogenic rooftops in the city, especially at sunset.

The cocktails are excellent, creative, and perfectly balanced — ideal for a date night, happy hour, or weekend hangout. The atmosphere feels elegant yet laid-back, with great music and a cool crowd.

The only downside is the food menu, which is pretty limited. It’s mostly pinsa-style pizzas, hummus boards, and light bites — tasty, but not much variety if you’re looking for a full meal.

If you want great drinks, a stunning Empire State Building view, and a relaxed rooftop vibe, this is definitely the place to go. Just come hungry for the view, not for the food.

📍 Sandbar Rooftop – 152 W 26th St, New York, NY
🥂 Top NYC Rooftop Bar with Empire State Building View
🍕 Small food menu (mostly pizzas & shareables)
🌇 Perfect for cocktails, photos, and sunsets

text.text:
We hosted our engagement party earlier this year at Ms. Kim’s Karaoke in Manhattan, and it was an unforgettable experience. The staff were incredibly accommodating, opening for us on a Sunday (when they’re usually closed) and giving us the freedom to shape the event without any pressure around food or logistics. There were some a/v hiccups with the mic, but the staff worked diligently to try and fix the issues for us.

We want to give a huge shoutout to JV, the bartender, who was not only super friendly and helpful but also jumped in as a fantastic singer to keep the energy going. Toki was amazing, helping us organize all the details in advance, and the catering team was efficient and thoughtful, even helping us figure out the best tabling setup for food. Every part of the team worked seamlessly to make the event a success.

The space itself is classy, polished, and perfect for a group gathering. The karaoke selection comes straight from YouTube, so there was something for all of our guests who wanted to sing. Overall, the whole team at Ms. Kim's made our night smooth, fun, and memorable. We highly recommend Ms. Kim’s for your next event.

text.text:
I chose to celebrate my birthday at Amber Room this October and unfortunately had one of the most disappointing experiences I’ve ever had at a hospitality venue.

I booked my table through a promoter named Pop (Instagram: @popteagle) and paid a $1,500 deposit, which he confirmed receiving. He also confirmed that my group was booked for a $3,500 minimum table. However, at the last moment, Pop changed his word and tried to move us to the DJ table with a new minimum of $4,000. We declined and reiterated that our agreement was for the $3,500 table.

When we arrived, Amber Room’s staff appeared completely uncoordinated with the promoter. The front desk even had our table listed as a $5,000 minimum. After some back-and-forth, they agreed to honor the $3,500 minimum as originally confirmed.

Unfortunately, when the night ended and our bill arrived, the staff increased the minimum to $4,000 and added an inflated gratuity—even though we were explicitly told gratuity would be left to our discretion. To make matters worse, Amber Room refused to apply our $1,500 deposit, claiming that Pop was not affiliated with them and should have never taken the payment.

Essentially, we were told the promoter acted independently and that the venue would not take responsibility, leaving us out $1,500 and forced to pay an inflated bill. Pop later attempted to justify this by saying the deposit was a “promoter’s tip,” something that was never discussed or agreed upon.

This entire experience felt dishonest and exploitative. What was meant to be a joyful birthday celebration turned into an incredibly stressful and disappointing night.

I strongly advise others to avoid booking tables at Amber Room through any promoter—or better yet, to avoid this establishment altogether. Their lack of coordination, accountability, and transparency makes it far too easy for customers to be taken advantage of.

text.text:
WOW Karaoke in Koreatown is an absolute blast, and I was genuinely impressed with the entire experience. From the moment you walk in, the vibrant, modern decor and colorful lighting set the stage for a fun night. We were able to get a private room right away with no wait, which was a fantastic start.

The service was top-notch throughout the night. The staff was incredibly friendly and attentive, making sure we had everything we needed without being intrusive. The song selection system is tablet-based, giving you access to a massive and up-to-date library of songs in different languages, including English, Korean, and Chinese. While there's a heads-up that the YouTube integration can sometimes be a little glitchy, we found plenty of options and the sound system in the room was excellent.

The atmosphere in the private rooms is perfect for a group outing. The rooms are clean, spacious, and feature cool lighting that adds to the vibe. We also ordered some food, and it definitely exceeded expectations for a karaoke bar; the fries and rice cakes were particularly good.

Be aware that on Friday and Saturday nights, the venue is 21+ and requires a bottle package after 8 PM, which can make it a bit pricey. However, for the quality of the experience, from the high-tech rooms to the outstanding service, it felt worth it for a great night out with friends. I’ll definitely be coming back.

The atmosphere in the private rooms is perfect for a group outing. The rooms are clean, spacious, and feature cool lighting that adds to the vibe. We also ordered some food, and it definitely exceeded expectations for a karaoke bar; the fries and rice cakes were particularly good.

Be aware that on Friday and Saturday nights, the venue is 21+ and requires a bottle package after 8 PM, which can make it a bit pricey. However, for the quality of the experience, from the high-tech rooms to the outstanding service, it felt worth it for a great night out with friends. I’ll definitely be coming back.

text.text: Petite Disco is quite possibly my favorite nightlife venue in NYC. The decor is enchanting and the interior design impeccable. text.text:
Absolutely the Worst Experience in NYC

I recently had the unfortunate experience of visiting Petite Disco, and I can confidently say it's the worst place in New York City. Having lived in this vibrant city for 16 years and produced numerous events, I thought I'd give this venue a chance based on a promoter friend's recommendation. Big mistake.

From the moment I stepped inside, the atmosphere was hostile. The staff, including the bouncers and coat check attendants, treated guests with blatant disrespect. Rather than a warm welcome, I was met with verbal hostility that left me shocked. It felt as if the staff was more interested in exerting power than providing a positive experience.

The physical and verbal abuse I witnessed was appalling. I was disheartened to see how poorly patrons were treated, and it was clear that this establishment prioritizes aggression over hospitality.

I can't stress enough how terrible my experience was. This place not only failed to meet basic expectations of service but also created an environment that felt unsafe and unwelcoming. I would never recommend Petite Disco to anyone, and frankly, I believe it should be closed down. Save yourself the trouble and avoid this disaster of a venue at all costs.

text.text:
(Please read so you can be well informed when you consider booking  an event)

I had been to the Inkwell Grown Friday's Afterwork club numerous times in the past years and had enjoyed myself. So when my husband was going to be 60 this year I decided it would be a nice place to celebrate.  I purchased my first two sections on July 8, 2025, then due to positive rsvp responses I brought another section on September 8, 2025.   On Sept. 24 (10 days before the event) Kevin, owner of inkwell productions called and informed me the rooftop venue was not available for the date I paid for(Oct.3, 2025). I had to chose between changing locations to some place on 133rd or change the date to the next weekend.  Neither one of those worked for me as I guests flying in to attend the event and also I had no desire to move my party uptown. It would change all of my plans of how I was getting to the venue. I asked Kevin for a refund since i could no longer have my party at the location or on the date i paid for, essentially they had canceled my party. He informed me that there are no refunds and that when I brought the tickets I agreed that "SECTIONS ARE SUBJECT TO CHANGE"
I stated that by definition a section is within a building of the same location/address. No persons buying tickets would take that to mean that their section would be moved 4.9 miles from the original address. Long story short he/his wife refused to refund my money, so due to lack of funds and the lack of time and under duress I moved the party to their 133rd street location.  When I arrived at the site, I was devastated to see that it was an actual restaurant.  There were tables and waite staff and people their to dine. They used outdoor like sofas and little cocktail tables to make a VIP section. The restaurant tables were close to the back of the sofa The lights were all on, traffic was visible and audible to us. It was very awkward as the people who were dining were looking at us as we were attempting to party  like we were at a club while they dined.  This was not a club, nor did it attempt to create a club atmosphere.  Then to make the night even worse, Jay , Inkwell manager comes over at 0956pm and tells me that Kevin only paid for us to have the section until 10p. and that we can stay at the restaurant but we no longer have VIP section. I informed him that we supposed to have it till 11p. He said sorry nope. I text Kevin and he said Jay was correct I could stay on the premises,  no more sections though.
This whole situation was caused by Inkwell double selling space.  I asked Kevin why wasn't the place availablefor Oct. 3 now,  when it was available when brought the sections as early as July and as  late as Sept 8. He said they rented out the entire rooftop space for a private party.  They did this knowing that people had already rented and paid money for the same space.
So yes Inkwell throw good parties but beware that if you book, you may not end up at the place or on the date you paid for.
Section are subject to change mean you can be absolutely anywhere according to their understanding.

RESPONSE. I never said that I didnt read the disclaimer,  I stated that section change doesn't equate to building change to me.
Also I purchased  3 VIP sections, which came with wings and cupcakes and  which was sufficient to accommodate my guest.There was no upgrade.  We were actually downgraded. We went from a  rooftop night club to a sidewalk patio at a Mexican restaurant.  All drink were paid for by myself and. my guest.
I also hope you sent the surveillance of me in the corner visually being comforted by my friends  due to being distraught at  seeing that the replacement venue was a freaking Mexican restaurant.  I also hope you have the video of Jay approaching me at 0956 telling me I had to move from that section, when my RECEIPTS says 5 to 11.  So yes let us all send our evidence to the the credit card company.

Inkwell Productions believing that a sidewalk patio area in a restaurant is equivalent to their rooftop nightclub is incredulous

We did the BYOB package, which was a fair price at $51 per person for a group of 11. I recommend contacting them in advance to let them know if you’re running late or if anything changes—especially when planning with a large group, as things can come up.
Thank you all for being so great and for making my 30th birthday celebration special!

I did the open bar package and it was great for a group celebration. It was a bit pricey but for the venue and birthday discount available I would definitely go with Space for an event. The service was great, I brought a cake and they were able to hold it and bring it out at the end of the reservation. The staff was very responsive and made sure to make my birthday the best possible. The food was great to share with people, and came fast after ordering. My only comment is that I believe there should be more than one bartender, especially at peak times like the weekend. With the package including 2 hours, a few of us spent a considerable amount of the time waiting for drinks, when it would’ve been preferable to use it in the room to sing. Overall, we all thoroughly enjoyed it, and it was the first time hosting a birthday event where every guest (including myself) urged for a repeat.

text.text:
This gaming lounge is honestly a gem in the city. The equipment is top-notch—high-powered PCs, smooth consoles, lightning-fast internet, and chairs you can sink into for hours. But what makes this place stand out isn’t just the gear, it’s the people who run it.

Tee is always on point, making sure the setups are running perfectly and keeping the energy high. You can tell Tee genuinely cares about everyone having the best possible experience, whether you’re here to grind all night or just play a few casual rounds.

And then there’s Cheryl, usually holding it down behind the bar. She’s the kind of person who makes you feel welcome right away. Always friendly, quick with a drink, and somehow remembers what everyone likes—she adds a personal touch that makes the lounge feel like home.

Between the atmosphere, the community, and the staff who really go above and beyond, this lounge has become my go-to spot. I’ve brought friends here who instantly loved it, and now it’s part of our routine. If you’re into gaming (or even just want a cool place to hang out), this is the place to be.

text.text:
We recently visited and found it to be highly deceptive and ultimately a huge disappointment. The advertising was fantastic, promising an incredible experience with claims of "25,000 games" and multiple levels filled with billiards and other game tables.
Upon arrival, our excitement quickly turned to frustration. We paid a admission charge of $40 plus tax for two people, only to discover that the place was nothing like the pictures. The advertised billiard table was rickity, and no chalk for cues. The numerous other game tables were nowhere to be found unless booked for a private event. The general access areas were small and completely inadequate for even playing the basic board games they had on offer.
We literally walked in, looked around, and realized there was nothing to do. We immediately decided to leave and asked for a refund, which the staff outright refused, despite the clear misrepresentation in their marketing.
This place is a complete rip-off. Save your money and time, as the reality of the bar doesn't match the grand picture they paint online. I would definitely not recommend this place to anyone.

text.text: I'm probably overrating this because we went on a slow night, and only had some apps and drinks, but the waitress and the bartender as well as the food and drinks were very good to exceptional. To start with the service I asked for a drink that was not on the menu ( a Russian mule not anything that exotic) and even though I ordered something else, the waitress and bartender looked it up and brought me one without me even ordering it. It was very good and they did not even charge me. I highly recommend the paper plane drink that was on the menu. My Italian buddy and his wife ordered one each and they were very good. Bourbon and aperol base who would have thought? The ladies ordered some Mediterranean plate with hummus and chips and the waitress noticed we were running low on chips and replenished - love it when the waiting staff is and on the ball! The next exceptional part was the pork belly sliders. Maybe I am partial to pork belly anything. But they the first ones were so succulent We ordered another round. The second ones were not as moist and generous, but still very good. Atmosphere was upscale casual was sitting nicely arranged for small groups towards the windows and larger gathering spaces around rows of comfortable couches. We went during the week in the evening so the vibe was chill, with cool music that was not disruptive to conversation and lighting That was just right relaxed but not too dark. Great evening overall

text.text: Skip this place. The views are great and it's very comfortably set up, and they even have good happy hour prices. The service, though, is just awful, and will wreck your experience. We got drinks delivered after about ten minutes, which was fine, but we also ordered chips and guac (so as not to drink on an empty stomach) and waited 25 minutes for it. The first time I asked our waiter it was quite obvious that he hadn't put the order in. The second time I asked he barely acknowledged the question. In the middle of all of this I asked for two waters. Our waiter spent a fair amount of time chatting at the bar while enjoying his own water...after another five minutes I just went up to the bar and got them myself. When the chips and guac finally arrived (by which time my ice-cold Manhattan was tepid) it was a ridiculously-presented plate, see pic below. We closed out our check and the waiter added a 20 percent tip for himself and told me it was standard. I pointed out that the menu indicated that a tip was standard for parties of six only. Caught red-handed, he removed the tip and tossed the bill down. No apologies for terrible service; he simply tried to get away with what he usually does, I guess. We won't be back!

text.text:
I hate to be the negative review on a business i genuinely think is trying to do a good job. Front staff was really awesome, friendly and welcoming.

The biggest gripe I had was with the taller bartender on the night of 1/3. black and curly short hair. Dude had the most disheartening attitude I’ve ever seen in such a positive environment like a karaoke bar. Miserable. It’s the service industry, atleast fake it. From the get go, inquiring about drinks, he just didn’t seem to want to help. He created a very unwelcoming environment for me and my friends. I still treated him nice but jeez.

Next gripe, the open karaoke set-up is less than ideal. The same 3-4 people were up on stage every single song. *But Rai, why didn’t you request a song yourself?* ahaha we did. Me and my friends requested songs, mind you, it costs $3 dollars per song with a minimum card purchase of $11 EACH TIME. For people who don’t carry cash, it’s a bit obsolete in 2025. It felt like there was favoritism, but I’m doubtful. I think the system is just bad. I think if you’re charging for a song, there should be an option to pay a dollar more or something to bump your song up the queue a bit. Or, alternatively, just let there be a dedicated song manager to mix it up a bit. It got so boring watching people never leave the stage singing 5 songs and not knowing the words for 4 of them.

I know I sound bitter, but we waited nearly 3 hours and never had a chance. Luckily there was another bartender, shorter guy, who allowed us to get a refund for the songs we paid for. They were really kind, and that made me feel a little better. Ultimately we still gave up and left.

Closing thoughts: the owners and front staff are awesome, and they have booths so next time I’d try the private booths for my group.

text.text:
I decided to go to karaoke for the first time on my birthday. After looking up a plethora of karaoke spots, this is the one I chose and I’m glad I did. I was 100% satisfied.

The reservation process was kind’ve tricky. I started on their website then they contacted me via text.

I made sure to ask as many questions which they answered all of them. Everything was transparent. No hidden fees.

When I arrived they greeted me and had my reservation ready. The room was a good size for my guests. They gave us complimentary water.

Our guided gave us steps to get the karaoke started and told us how to add songs to the queue. Also, the checked in on us like 3 times. The system was easy to use. If you want to get the most bang for your buck then have all the songs you want ready before you arrive.

The song selection was pretty good. Was missing some songs but they have songs up to 2025. There’s also a section of their website to submit songs. Unfortunately it was down the weekend I went. I was still able to get the other songs I wanted.

All my friends were able to enjoy themselves and pick out songs. It was really fun.

When it came time to pay, the price was pretty good for the group. It was under $50. Be mindful for card the max they can take is 2 cards. The price included tax and gratuity. Which I was fine with.

We didn’t feel like drinking. There was no mandatory drink requirement or hidden fees. Which I liked.

I would definitely come back. Didn’t take any pics but I’ll add some videos from our session. Don’t judge lol.

text.text:
Came in alone last night and I was just hoping to have a drink and maybe not feel invisible. One bartender, longer hair, was really welcoming at first I really liked his singing and his drinks were really not that well mixed but, honestly, he was really cute so I probably read too much into it. As the night went on, that warmth faded, and it didn’t seem like my very mild advances were reciprocated. Fair enough, but it still felt awkward. The prices are a little too much for the quality of service and drinks anyways.

I also met a bald man who was handsome who seemed part of my type of community. In a painfully earnest moment, I was genuinely hoping he’d become my best friend after all we rap. That energy was very much not returned, and the interaction ended up feeling smug on his end and deeply embarrassing on mine.

I’m fully aware this makes me sound pathetic, and it probably is. Still, the overall vibe felt very insiders-only, which isn’t great when you show up solo and try. The bar itself seems cool — just maybe better if you come with friends or emotional armor. Also smug bartenders is not a great way to run a bar.

text.text:
Trailer Park Lounge
271 W. 23rd St.
New York, NY 10011

Frozen Strawberry Margarita
Mac N’ Cheese Bites
Mother In Law’s Revenge Burger

Located in the Chelsea District is a bar and grill with vintage décor.

Let’s check out the cocktail and 2-course meal I explored.

Frozen Strawberry Margarita:
This frozen margarita may be small in size, but strong enough for a nice solid buzz.

The frosty cocktail is mixed with tequila, strawberries, with salt along the rim, a lime, and an umbrella.

Mac N’ Cheese Bites:
This appetizer includes three fairly large balls.

They are breaded on the outside and packed with macaroni and cheese on the inside; also included is a side of queso and salsa.

Mother In Law’s Revenge Burger:
The toppings on this specialty burger include cheese, chili, jalapenos and sour cream.

Although it was loaded with condiments, it was still easy to hold.  It has quite a kick.

In addition to a decent meal, the vintage decorations truly caught my eye.

There is a variety of life size mannequins and statues.

The retro machines that are no longer operable, but really neat to admire.

It’s like visiting an antique shop except you cannot purchase anything.

Seating options extend beside the traditional bar or dining tables.

The bartender works both the bar and dining area, so please be patient with the service.  Otherwise, it’s a truly classic spot with decent eats.

text.text:
We came with friends on Tuesday night to support a friend in the competition. The show itself was great, the bar staff were super pleasant, the comedy was fun, and the host was great and friendly.

The only real letdown was the DJ. Instead of just handling the music, he kept talking over the comedians during their sets, which was distracting for both the performers and the audience. At one point, the host even asked him to let the comedians “do their thing,” but he still kept interrupting and even argued back. It definitely took away from the otherwise great vibe.

Reserved this place for a celebration. I really appreciate the specialized greeting card they gave when we arrived. They gave us seats fairly close to the window which is nice. The host was very welcoming and had good vibes overall. Our server was very helpful also.

I really liked the amaretto sour. The calamares was good too but maybe some bread would've been nice with it. After the drinks and appetizers were served, it took some time for our mains to be served. Maybe it was a busy day for them. The Bolognese and brick pressed chicken were alright.

Overall a pleasant experience.

text.text:
Dining at Centurion New York was nothing short of a dream. The moment you step inside, it feels as if you’re tasting—just for an instant—the exhilarating, almost surreal dimension of New York itself. The atmosphere, the design, the energy… everything comes together to create an unforgettable experience.

A very special recognition goes to Chef Rodrigo, whose culinary mastery elevated the evening to another level. Each dish was better than the one before it, crafted with precision, creativity, and a deep understanding of flavor. Truly remarkable.

We also want to highlight Gabriel, our server, who brought a warm, familiar welcome to the table. His elegance, professionalism, and genuine attentiveness reflected the high standards of a place like this. The entire staff operated with the same level of excellence—gracious, polished, and perfectly aligned with the spirit of Centurion.

This was more than a dinner; it was an experience. One that captures the magic of New York in every detail. We left deeply impressed and grateful.

text.text:
I can for sure tell that this was one of the best dining experiences in New York. I had been looking for so long for a special place to celebrate my friend's belated birthday, and I'm so happy that we booked here.

Amazing food, fresh and tasty, so nicely presented; all details were impeccable. To start, we ordered tuna tartare, carrot arancini, stracciatella and fig salad, a selection of cheese, and kale salad, and everything was delightful. Even though we were already full, we ordered mushroom pasta with cognac puree with a side of delicata squash and, of course, French fries—also super tasty. We finished our experience with a very refreshing dessert, Chestnut Mont Blanc, and if you are a huge fan of extremely sweet desserts, the chocolate eclair would be a great choice.

Amazing ambiance, outstanding view, and calm music made our dining experience great. We are definitely looking forward to our next visit, probably to try their pre-fixe menu experience.

Regular NY prices, 2 of us spent $500 together with tip.

(They are not refreshing the table between meals, meaning not crumbing, and that is the only thing that bothers me a bit.)

The egg sandwich is good, did think it has a little too much small but still good and hash browns are extremely crispy. Waiter did forget my water after telling her 4 times but that’s ok. The pina colada drink was good but tasted more on the lemon side. Did sit on a high seat which was kinda uncomfortable. If I had a normal sitting table maybe my experience would be much better. Made a reservation on open table. Cool open rooftop area. Overall was a good brunch day for pictures. This is somewhere I came once and I’m good so won’t be coming back. Go for the vibes and the atmosphere. One and done experience.

text.text:
Magic Hour Rooftop is truly one of a kind! Such a beautiful spot with a stunning carousel decorated with flowers – it feels absolutely magical. The drinks are just wow, and I definitely recommend trying the cocktail bouquet, especially the biggest one – it’s an experience in itself.

The food is very good, though the portions are on the smaller side, which is fine for this type of place. What I really love is how the vibe changes depending on where you sit: on one side it feels more like a lively lunch party with a DJ, and on the other side it’s romantic with a more intimate atmosphere. The DJ playing during my last visit was fantastic and really added to the night (@djkizzy)

I’ve been here several times already, and everyone I’ve ever brought absolutely loved it. I’ll definitely keep coming back – highly recommend!

text.text:
Hard PASS. After a long waited 3 ish years of waiting to visit Magic Hour, I was finally able to make my trip align with visiting Magic Hour during their themed seasons!

You all posted on social media stating the new winter theme would be up by Nov 26 and to click the link in bio to make a reservation to come see the new set up. I clicked. I reserved. I came & saw nothing.

Of course once my party gets there for our reservation, we get told “it’s getting set up”…that’s not what it says online but fine. We sit and wait, probably close to 10 mins before our waitress approaches us….. but no problem they could be busy with the 3 small groups of people already seated… Let’s enjoy happy hour and move on. Happy hour is 3 drinks and 3 drinks only…. Okay sure, let’s get the holiday drink featured on social media that also appears on the happy hour menu and the actual menu as the same name. I look at the drink I order and it looks completely different. Confused I asked about the cranberry & rosemary, she said “sure” as if to grab these things for me to make it myself. She comes back with cranberries in a cup and pours them into my drink……….um? I asked again, “No it’s different” is what the waitress tells me. If the holiday drink has the same name on happy hour (Winter White Spritz) why am I getting a completely different drink? The waitress was super nice the entire experience but I think the language barrier really played a part in not understanding my frustration with the entire situation.

I asked the waitress if there was anything else to the bar because the social media page makes it seem like there are many different areas and get told no. Come to find out there are three different bars for Magic Hour and though we asked several times about the winter theme for today, no one seems concerned or cared for the fact that this is the only reason I made this reservation as they POSTED online about it being launched on the 26th…the day they stated to click the link to make a reservation for to view the new theme.

The false advertisement on both ends for the drinks and the winter set up completion was a complete disappointment.

The only great thing we did get to “experience” were the sliders. The chicken & ribeye sliders were delicious, but not worth the trouble we went through while being there.

Disappointed to say the least. On top of watching other parties that came after us get serviced quickly and with excitement, it was such a drag to be there as much as we tried to enjoy the moment. The time it took to be serviced versus those after us. The same drink but not the same drink between the happy hour menu and regular menu. They forgot one of the food orders we placed and was confused on whether we even ordered it…we did. & the lack of understanding the frustration when being asked about the area, the space and the theme we only showed up for. It’s a great concept, but it seemed like you needed to already know someone that worked there to truly get a great experience.

text.text:
The place is stunning — the food and atmosphere are truly five-star quality, and it’s clear that most of the staff work hard to make guests feel welcome.
However, our experience at the bar didn’t match the same level of care. The bartender seemed distracted and lacked the empathy and attention one expects in such a highly rated venue.
I came here with my wife to enjoy what’s known as one of the best rooftop spots in the city, and it’s a shame that one person’s attitude can affect an otherwise wonderful experience.
This place has everything to be perfect if all staff shared the same level of professionalism as the rest of the team.

text.text: If you, a loved one, a friend, etc. loves history, especially military or American history, you cannot beat the experience at the Intrepid Museum. From its collection of military aircraft from several decades, to housing one of the US space shuttles, to the friendliness and knowledge of the museum's staff and volunteers, this is a great experience. Helps too that the museum is somewhat kid friendly and there's lots to see and do. Don't miss the neighboring World War 2 era submarine to make it a complete day. Plus, amazing views of the Hudson and the city while on deck!

text.text:
Today I visited a gallery and, honestly, I was deeply disappointed. Very few of the works carried real quality and soul – most of the paintings and sculptures looked machine-made, without organic lines, without the human touch. As a professional artist with more than 25 years of international experience in the arts, I was also surprised by the behavior of the person working there. Instead of showing respect and willingness to share information about the artworks or the artists, I was met with arrogance and indifference.

A gallery should be a space that gives soul, attention, and love – a place where artists and their creations are valued and appreciated. What I felt instead was a cold, plastic approach. I feel sorry for the artists whose works are displayed in such an environment, because their art deserves much more. It makes me wonder: are such galleries serving art at all, or are they simply tools for the wealthy to play their “tax-free” games, while the true spirit of art is left behind?

I look forward to doing more business with them in the near future.

It commemorates Evacuation Day, the day that British troops left the island of Manhattan after the conclusion of the American Revolution.  On November 25, 1783, the British departed and George Washington triumphantly led the Continental Army down through Manhattan to the Battery.  Dedicated in 1856, this statue is the oldest sculpture in the New York City Parks collection.  The statue originally stood in a traffic island in the middle of 14th Street until it was moved to its present location in 1930.

Once you pass the trees, there is an abundant hospital covered by the ivy. It is so beautiful that I took a seat by the building and looked at it for a long time. The smallpox hospital could appear in "Wuthering Heights." If you like Wuthering Heights' atmosphere, I recommend visiting here. On the other side of the hospital, you can see Manhattan and its skyscrapers. The UN headwater stands like a wall. After looking at the beautiful construction for a long time, you should take yourself to the Franklin D. Roosevelt Four Freedoms State Park.

text.text:
A haunting, unforgettable stop on Roosevelt Island. The Smallpox Memorial Hospital, now a striking ruin, is a powerful piece of hidden New York history. Walking the grounds and gazing at the crumbling stone façade was surreal—like stepping into a ghost story carved into the skyline.

We got there via the Roosevelt Island Tram, which alone is worth the trip for its sweeping views over the East River. After exploring the island, we took the subway back to Midtown, but the memory of the hospital lingered.

Built in 1856, this Gothic Revival structure was the first major hospital in the U.S. dedicated solely to treating smallpox—a disease that once terrorized the country. At its peak, it treated thousands, and though it eventually closed in the 1950s, its bones remain as a quiet tribute to the battles fought there—both medical and human.

The site is not open for entry, but it’s beautifully preserved from the outside, especially near Southpoint Park, and offers stunning views with a heavy, poignant air. History lovers, photographers, and the quietly curious will find it unforgettable.

text.text:
I watched Maybe Happy Ending at the Belasco Theatre yesterday, sitting in the very last row on the first floor. Even from the back, the view was excellent — the theater is intimate enough that you can clearly see the stage and feel fully immersed.

The show itself was absolutely delightful. The story, music, and performances were heartfelt and refreshing, and the emotional moments landed beautifully. I walked out feeling uplifted and genuinely happy.

Belasco is a smaller, charming historic theater, and its cozy scale actually makes the experience even better. You don’t need a front-row seat to enjoy the show — every seat feels close.

Overall, an unexpectedly wonderful evening. I’d definitely recommend this show and venue.

text.text: One of two Chelsea outposts of the great Hauser & Wirth gallery. The exhibition on David Smith's late sculptures was an eye opener if you are used to his "standard" sculptures in shiny bare steel. These late sculptures are a lot more experimental than I would have expected in terms of forms and colors. The 5th floor skylit gallery is a great space for these sculptures. The building was designed by Selldorf Architects whose other New York projects include the Neue Galerie, the Frick Collection renovation, and One Domino Square in Williamsburg. I especially like the staircase.

text.text:
A breathtaking and unforgettable experience!!

We had a phenomenal and deeply moving visit to the 9/11 Museum Workshop. From start to finish, it was a unique, real-life experience that exceeded all expectations. Being able to touch actual artifacts from Ground Zero brought the history to life in a way that no other museum can replicate. It felt personal, raw, and incredibly powerful.

Our guide, Gary, was outstanding — detailed, passionate, and deeply knowledgeable, sharing firsthand experiences that added a truly human and emotional layer to the tour. You could feel his heart in every word.

This isn’t just a museum — it’s a tribute, a memory, and a lesson, all in one. Absolutely worth every penny. We highly, highly recommend it to anyone visiting New York.

text.text:
Incredibly moving experience. We took our pre-teen children to the museum workshop, and the powerful story telling and visuals (both 2D and 3D) helped our children understand the event, in a way that is suitable for the whole family. Gary spent endless hours on the images, collecting artefacts donations, recording audio clips and creating the workshop and keep it running.

The workshop location is located closer to midtown. In traffic-heavy New York, it makes a big difference for tourists like us.

This is a profoundly transformative experience—one that opens the eyes of both children and adults to the consequences of ignorance, fear, and misunderstanding. It compels us to reflect not just on our personal choices, but on our roles within a global community. The events of 9/11 remind us that innocence and guilt are rarely absolute. Every nation, every individual carries history, responsibility, and the power to change. In today’s interconnected world, this lesson is more urgent than ever: our actions ripple outward, affecting lives far beyond our own. By understanding this, we can raise a generation that chooses empathy over indifference, wisdom over assumption, and peace over vengeance.

text.text:
A visual journey of grief, hope, and humanity

An unforgettable, emotional experience—the story after the story.

This visit was deeply impactful, shedding light on the human spirit during the Ground Zero “Recovery Period.” A huge bonus: our guide was none other than Gary Marlon Suson, a masterful artist whose lens captured the soul of that time with reverence and grace. His storytelling and photography bring history to life in a way that is both respectful and moving.

A must-see for anyone visiting NYC.

text.text:
We stumbled across a tiny sign on our last visit to NYC, and when it came time to plan our next trip, my husband booked this experience.
It is a tiny little studio near Chelsea markets (if you didn't know it was there, you would miss it) full of so much history.
The photographer Garry Marlon Susan has done a brilliant job displaying his photos and artefacts in a tasteful and respective way.
We were able to touch and hold artefacts from that day, making it even more emotional.
I was moved by the stories and would highly recommend.
Thank you, Garry, for sharing this with us.

Very cool café!

My wife had the turkey provolone sandwich and really loved it — she was very pleasantly surprised by how good it was.

The place is quite unique, located right in the middle of the MET, and it’s surprisingly spacious. It also feels very original, with a coffee stand — like an open food truck — that even serves ice cream.

You can stop by for lunch (sandwiches and salads) or just enjoy a snack like cookies, croissants, or pains au chocolat.

A great spot to take a break during your visit!

text.text:
It was a special moment for me here at the Great Lawn in Central Park. Both my parents were in town to visit me, and it was a special feeling to show them my stomping grounds for the past 3 years.

We went to Citarella, got a bunch of ingredients to make our own charcuterie board, and just had a DAY. I’m at the age now where my parents are more friends than parents, which is a beautiful phase of life.

We dug into our build-your-own charcuterie and people watched, took naps, and just enjoyed each other's company. Central park never ceases to amaze me, as it provides such a breath of fresh air right smack in the middle of the busiest city in the world.

So grateful for Nanc and Steve.

text.text:
Verdi Square is a small triangular-shaped public space just where Broadway intersects Amsterdam Ave. in the Upper West Side.

Here you will find a small garden with flower beds and the architecturally interesting 72nd Street Station. There is also a nice monument to composer Giuseppe Verdi which was unveiled here in 1906.

The Verdi Monument is the works of Sicilian sculptor Pasquale Civiletti. It depicts a middle-aged Verdi in suit and holding a folded overcoat in his left arm. He is standing on a round pedestal surrounded by Falstaff, Leonora, Aida and Otello, four important characters from Verdi's opera works.

In the end, Verdi Square is a small location that is interesting to spend a few minutes enjoying if you happen to be in this are of the Upper West Side.

text.text:
Very cool and chill place. Went here when me and my bf were pretty high, had amazing conversations with a beautiful view of the driveway and Roosevelt island. We were observing the intimidating black gate with its sharp spikes. I remembered a story when one of my former classmates had his leg become a kebab on a similar spike when he was going over the same gate when escaping from school. We took a nice photo there to commemorate the evening and the stunning view. On our way out, we discovered that this exact gate at the entrance was locked, no other exit was available at 11pm. I believe the responsible person didn't check if anyone was still in there. FYI, the park closes at 10.00pm. If you stay later, you are at risk of getting kebabed. So, being sky high and scared, we decided to play Spiderman. My bf literally jumped over the gate when I got stuck on top. The trash bins came to my rescue, I jumped on one of them, and then I was beautifully taken down from it.
Since that romantic moment, we broke up, and now he is my ex. Despite the fact that we were closed inside and had to figure it out, I give this park 5 stars because of its cozy vibe and the beautiful memories that warm me up until today 🧡

text.text:
I've eaten 30 rotisserie chickens in the past 2 months, and I'm exhausted. You asked for my hustle, I gave you my heart.

I'm mostly exhausted from working a full time job while doing this, which has become another full time job in itself. When I started the journey to find the best rotisserie chicken in the country, there was so much I didn't know:

1) I had no idea people would actually care and be as invested in this series as they are, which is kind of cool.

2) There are more grocery stores selling a hot & ready rotisserie chicken than I ever could have imagined. I have so much work left to do.

I ate the $25.99 tissi from Ouri's here at St. Katherine's Park, and it was a nice place to just sit and eat some bird. A couple basketball courts, playgrounds, ample seating, and bathrooms that are in some desperate need of some TLC. It was a vibe.

The bird was actually bomb, but charging $25.99 for a rotisserie chicken should be a war crime. I will never buy it again.

I'm taking the night train to Boston tonight to try Market Basket, Star Market, Stop & Shop, and a few other grocery stores. I also need to get to Texas, the Midwest, and Southern California before I wrap this journey up.

I'm tired, but my momma always told me you don't start something until you finish it.

IG @jnov_
Tiktok @jnov__

text.text:
A peaceful hidden gem in the heart of Manhattan

Paley Park is one of my favorite small escapes in Midtown. It’s a tiny pocket park, but the moment you step inside, the atmosphere changes completely. The sound of the waterfall blocks out the city noise, making it feel calm and almost private.

I visited when the weather was quite cold, and although I imagine it would be even nicer on a warm day, it was still a great place to sit for a moment and enjoy the quiet. The seating is comfortable, a cozy atmosphere, and it’s a perfect spot to take a break, drink a coffee, or pause between meetings.

For such a busy part of Manhattan, finding a place this peaceful is rare. Definitely worth stopping by.
